# RAPath US

> FDA Medical Device Regulatory Reference

Plain English reference for FDA medical device regulation — 510(k), PMA, De Novo, quality system, UDI, and post-market obligations.

RAPath is an independent open-source project providing plain English navigation aids for medical device regulatory requirements. Content is written with AI assistance and derived from publicly available official sources only. Always verify against official regulatory sources before making any regulatory decisions. Not legal or regulatory advice.

## Sections

- [Start Here](https://rapath.org/us/start-here/what-is-a-medical-device)
- [Pre-Market](https://rapath.org/us/pre-market/classification/how-classification-works)
- [Post-Market](https://rapath.org/us/post-market/overview)
- [Quality System (QMSR)](https://rapath.org/us/qms/overview)
- [Establishment & UDI](https://rapath.org/us/establishment-udi/registration-overview)
- [What's New](https://rapath.org/us/whats-new/regulatory-changes)

## Official Sources

- [FDA — Medical Devices](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices)
- [FD&C Act (eCFR)](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I)
- [21 CFR Title 21](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21)
- [FDA 510(k) Database](https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cdrh/cfdocs/cfpmn/pmn.cfm)
- [GUDID Database](https://accessgudid.nlm.nih.gov)

- [All Jurisdictions](https://rapath.org/#jurisdictions)
